reasoning: FIDE releases official rating lists on the 1st of each month. The 2026-04-12 rating was 2482. A 1% increase requires a rating of >= 2507. For 2026-04-19, the FIDE rating will be identical to the April 1 list (2482), virtually guaranteeing the condition is not met (p=0.02). For 2026-05-12, she would need to gain 25 rating points in a single month; recent data suggests she played in the American Cup in March (likely accounted for in her April rating) and such a leap in April alone is unlikely (0.05). In the medium-to-long term, Carissa is young, the US #1 woman, and has stated intentions to pursue chess professionally. Gaining 25 points over 1-3 years is plausible, leading to a steady increase in probability. By 2029-2036, there are huge uncertainties around plateauing (many top women historically hover around 2450-2500), retirement, and the risk that if she reaches 2507 she still might not make the Wikipedia Top 20 table due to rating inflation. Therefore, probabilities naturally increase but cap out at maximum uncertainty (0.50) for the most distant horizons according to instructions.
